topic: Kernel-verified Lean proof closing an open sorry in Erdős Problem 1150 (formal-conjectures)

artifacts.title: Lean 4 proof of erdos_1150.variants.parseval_lower_bound (Parseval lower bound for ±1 polynomials), kernel-verified

metadata.method: discrete Parseval via orthogonality of the (n+1)-th roots of unity: mean of |P|^2 over the roots = sum |a_k|^2 = n+1, so the max (hence the sup over the circle) is >= sqrt(n+1)

candidate_claims.assertion: The Lean 4 theorem `erdos_1150.variants.parseval_lower_bound` is proven and kernel-verified (axioms: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound; no sorryAx): for every polynomial P over ℂ of degree n whose coefficients (indices 0..n) all lie in {-1, 1}, the supremum of |P(z)| over the unit circle |z| = 1 is at least √(n+1). This closes a previously-open `sorry` (category: textbook) attached to Erdős Problem 1150 in google-deepmind/formal-conjectures.
caveats: This is the textbook Parseval lower-bound variant, not the open Erdős 1150 conjecture itself (the (1+c)√n improvement remains open).
Kernel-verified locally on lean4 v4.27.0 with no sorryAx; upstream PR to formal-conjectures is the external acceptance step and requires the maintainer's action.
